app_info.subtitle: Incident ops assistant
app_info.description: IncidentFlow helps teams investigate incidents from ChatGPT by summarizing incidents, correlating alerts, reading Slack incident context, checking external provider status, and performing read-only Kubernetes inspection through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only retrieves and summarizes IncidentFlow incident information such as status, timeline, affected services, and recommendations; it does not create, update, delete, send messages, or perform irreversible actions.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to IncidentFlow incident data and does not take actions in external systems or the open world.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not delete, overwrite, restart, scale, patch, send messages, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only computes correlations from alert objects supplied to the tool and returns grouped alerts, likely root cause, severity, and confidence without modifying any source system.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to caller-provided alert data and local IncidentFlow computation; it does not contact or act on external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not delete, overwrite, restart, scale, patch, send messages, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads public provider status information for supported providers such as GitHub or AWS and returns current status and incident summaries without modifying provider state.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to public read-only provider status pages and does not perform actions in external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not delete, overwrite, restart, scale, patch, send messages, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads recent Slack alert messages for incident analysis and does not post messages, update messages, delete messages, invite users, or change Slack workspace configuration.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to a configured Slack alert channel in the connected workspace and does not take actions in Slack or elsewhere.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not post, update, delete, invite users, change configuration, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads a Slack alert thread and parses replies, links, hypotheses, decisions, and resolution signals without posting or modifying Slack data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to reading a selected Slack thread in the connected workspace and does not take actions in Slack or elsewhere.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not post, update, delete, invite users, change configuration, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads a Slack incident thread and computes a summary for incident analysis; it does not execute commands, post messages, update messages, or modify Slack data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to reading a selected Slack thread and optional alert context in the connected workspace.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not post, update, delete, invite users, execute commands, change configuration, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool dispatches only allowlisted read-only Kubernetes inspection actions through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent, such as listing resources, reading pod details, reading redacted logs, and checking rollout status.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters authorized in IncidentFlow and only uses read-only agent inspection commands.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool checks Kubernetes agent connectivity and read-only permission health; it may query cluster reachability and permissions but does not modify resources.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters authorized in IncidentFlow and only performs read-only checks.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads Kubernetes namespaces, Pods, Deployments, Services, Events, and health signals through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent without modifying cluster state.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters authorized in IncidentFlow and only performs read-only inspection.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads Kubernetes resources in one allowed namespace, including Pods, Events, Deployments, Services, and health signals, without modifying resources.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool checks which read-only Kubernetes permissions are available through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent and does not change RBAC or cluster configuration.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters authorized in IncidentFlow and only checks read-only capability.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads Kubernetes agent registry status, version, heartbeat, and selected cluster identity without dispatching write commands or modifying cluster resources.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to IncidentFlow agent registry data and connected Kubernetes cluster metadata.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool lists Kubernetes namespaces visible through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent and does not modify namespaces or cluster configuration.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters authorized in IncidentFlow and only lists resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool lists Kubernetes Pods in an allowed namespace through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent and does not modify Pods or workloads.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads details for one Kubernetes Pod in an allowed namespace through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent and does not modify the Pod.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads redacted recent logs from a selected Kubernetes Pod through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent and does not modify Pods, containers, Deployments, or configuration.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow and only reads logs.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool lists Kubernetes Events in an allowed namespace through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent and does not modify Events or resources.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool lists Kubernetes Deployments in an allowed namespace and returns rollout and availability metadata without modifying Deployments.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool lists Kubernetes Services in an allowed namespace and returns service type, ports, selectors, and metadata without modifying Services.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads Kubernetes Deployment rollout status and returns readiness, availability, and rollout progress without restarting, scaling, patching, or modifying the Deployment.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool shows Kubernetes namespaces through automatic cluster resolution and does not modify namespaces or cluster configuration.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters authorized in IncidentFlow and only lists resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool shows Kubernetes Pods through automatic cluster resolution and returns current Pod status metadata without modifying Pods.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool reads Pod status and restart metadata to find Pods that are not running, not ready, crash looping, pending, failed, or recently restarted without modifying Pods.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool inspects a Kubernetes workload rollout, related Pods, health status, and redacted recent logs through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent without modifying the workload.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to connected Kubernetes clusters and namespaces authorized in IncidentFlow and only performs read-only inspection.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool never creates, updates, deletes, restarts, scales, patches, or modifies Kubernetes resources.

test_cases.description: Summarize an active IncidentFlow incident for an on-call engineer.
test_cases.user_prompt: Summarize incident inc_123 for me, including severity, current status, timeline, affected services, and recommended next steps.
test_cases.expected_output: A concise incident summary with severity, status, affected services, timeline highlights, and read-only remediation recommendations based on IncidentFlow incident data.

test_cases.description: Correlate multiple alert objects supplied by the user.
test_cases.user_prompt: Correlate these alerts and tell me whether they look related: API 5xx spike for checkout, database connection saturation for payments, and checkout latency p95 breach in the same five-minute window.
test_cases.expected_output: A correlation analysis that groups related alerts, identifies likely shared services or labels, estimates root cause and severity, and explains confidence without modifying any system.

test_cases.description: Read Slack alert context and summarize the related incident thread.
test_cases.user_prompt: Look at the latest production alert in our Slack alert channel and summarize the related thread for incident context.
test_cases.expected_output: A read-only summary of the relevant Slack alert and thread, including hypotheses, decisions, links, commands mentioned by engineers, and possible resolution signals.

test_cases.description: Find unhealthy Kubernetes pods through the connected IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show me unhealthy pods in the production cluster and highlight crash loops, pending pods, failed pods, and recent restarts.
test_cases.expected_output: A read-only Kubernetes health report listing unhealthy pods with namespace, status, readiness, restart count, and likely investigation hints.

test_cases.description: Analyze one Kubernetes workload without modifying the cluster.
test_cases.user_prompt: Analyze deployment/checkout-api in the production namespace and tell me whether the rollout and related pods look healthy.
test_cases.expected_output: A read-only workload analysis covering rollout status, related pods, unhealthy signals, recent redacted logs when available, and recommended next inspection steps.

negative_test_cases.description: Reject Slack write actions because IncidentFlow tools are read-only.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Post a message in the incident Slack channel saying the deployment is fixed.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The assistant should explain that IncidentFlow can read and summarize Slack incident context but cannot post, update, or delete Slack messages.

negative_test_cases.description: Reject Kubernetes mutation requests because the app only performs inspection.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Restart the checkout-api deployment and scale it to 10 replicas.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The assistant should refuse to perform the mutation and explain that the available Kubernetes tools are read-only inspection tools only.

negative_test_cases.description: Avoid triggering tools for tasks unrelated to incident investigation or connected operational data.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Book a meeting with the payments team tomorrow and email the customer a status update.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The assistant should not call IncidentFlow tools and should explain that the app does not schedule meetings or send email.
